name: pyannote.audio
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); requires HuggingFace token to accept model license; no API costs

auth.notes: HuggingFace token required to accept pyannote model license before download

performance.notes: Faster-than-realtime on GPU; ~3-5x real-time on CPU for typical audio

agent_readiness.notes: State-of-the-art speaker diarization library. Answers 'who spoke when?' for any audio file. Pipeline API: from pyannote.audio import Pipeline. ONNX-accelerated inference. Used with WhisperX for diarized transcription (who said what). Multiple speaker segmentation. Voice Activity Detection (VAD). Overlap speech detection. Used in meeting transcription, podcast analysis, media monitoring.

gotchas: HuggingFace license acceptance required — gated model
Performance degrades with >8 speakers or overlapping speech
Speaker number must be estimated or provided for best results
best_when: Meeting/podcast transcription with speaker labels ('John: ..., Mary: ...')
Combined with WhisperX for diarized speech-to-text
Media monitoring and content analysis by speaker
summary: pyannote.audio is the state-of-the-art Python library for speaker diarization — answering 'who spoke when?' for any audio. Used with WhisperX for complete 'who said what' transcription. ONNX-accelerated. Gated model requires HuggingFace license acceptance. Best for meeting transcription, podcast analysis, and building speaker-aware voice applications.
